acceptance_targets: [chapter_07_craft_001] PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:249-253 preserves the client's residue in the room and moves into the kiss without any sentence that explicitly says the sex is unrelated to Luz or otherwise morally clears Mira and Tavi in advance. FAIL if an exculpatory aside still appears before the kiss.

[chapter_07_prose_001] PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347-377 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:477-499 use no more than one 'He looked...' sentence total and render at least two of Tavi's state changes through nonvisual cues such as posture, breath, hand movement, or voice. PASS only if one of the comparison-led sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:189 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:253 is also rewritten into a more direct formulation. FAIL if the same scaffolds still carry consecutive emotional beats.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:249-253 preserves the client's residue in the room and moves into the kiss without any sentence that explicitly says the sex is unrelated to Luz or otherwise morally clears Mira and Tavi in advance. FAIL if an exculpatory aside still appears before the kiss.
findings.problem: Right before Mira kisses Tavi, the narration explicitly assures the reader that the desire is unrelated to the assaulted client who just left. That sentence works as a moral safety valve: it pre-clears the ethics of the scene instead of letting the recent injury, bodily residue, and compromised room remain active pressure. Given the constitution's sanitization standard, that is a craft failure, not a harmless clarification.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:249 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:251 so the room's contamination stays present without narratorial acquittal. Keep Luz's residue, the red-bin/sweet-rot context, and Mira's desire in the same frame, but remove the sentence that explicitly clears the sex of ethical contamination; let Mira choose the kiss under pressure rather than having the prose certify her innocence first.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347-377 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:477-499 use no more than one 'He looked...' sentence total and render at least two of Tavi's state changes through nonvisual cues such as posture, breath, hand movement, or voice. PASS only if one of the comparison-led sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:189 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:253 is also rewritten into a more direct formulation. FAIL if the same scaffolds still carry consecutive emotional beats.
findings.problem: The back half develops a noticeable sentence-engine tic. Major emotional turns keep arriving through the same comparison and observation scaffolds, especially repeated 'as if' constructions and repeated 'He looked...' entries into Tavi's state. The prose stays strong at the image level, but these recycled hinges start to flatten the chapter's most important turns into variations on the same move.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated scaffold sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:375, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:477, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:497. Keep Mira's close POV, but vary how she reads Tavi and the room: use posture, breath, hand pressure, voice break, spatial hesitation, or object interaction instead of repeatedly leaning on 'as if' comparison hinges or 'He looked...' sentence starts.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into biotech grime, erotic awkwardness, debt language, bureaucratic absurdity, and the tenderness of people maintaining one another under bad systems. When choosing between an elegant abstraction and an ugly specific detail, choose the specific detail unless omission itself heightens pressure.
aesthetic_risk_policy.edge_case_resolution: Use restraint only when aftermath, gesture, residue, or changed behavior keeps the full pressure visible. Do not convert morally compromising acts into self-exonerating reflection, and do not flatten exploitative sex, poverty, or violence into generic dystopian mood.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches injury, sex, class shame, or institutional cruelty and the language retreats into tasteful fog, rewrite until the bodily fact, immediate sensation, and social consequence are fully legible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms are reserved for legal testimony, managerial performance, deliberate emphasis, or characters whose formality is itself a tactic.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate inspection-room procedural chapters with crowded comic clinic scenes, quiet domestic intervals, erotic pressure, warehouse horror, and civic hearing sequences; consecutive chapters should differ in scene density, dialogue load, and emotional temperature so the book keeps changing how it moves.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, urban, bodily, and unsentimental; technical vocabulary is welcome when embedded in action and cost, not as decorative futurism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third-person close anchored to Mira Alves; the narration can infer social systems through what she notices, but it should not become omniscient exposition.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate crisp claim-note precision with longer, salt-wet, bodily sentences when feeling, memory, or city pressure breaches Mira's control.
